article_meta.title: Takeaways from the millions of newly released Epstein files 2 hours agoShareSaveSakshi VenkatramanandKwasi Gyamfi Asiedu

reasoning: The model exhibits several failure patterns: (1) It uses bias type labels not from the defined list ('Framing Bias', 'Emphasis Bias', 'Source Selection Bias', 'Labeling Bias' are all invented types). (2) It flags quoted/attributed text as author bias in items 2 and 3 (DOJ statements in quotes). (3) Several flagged items are questionable or not genuine author-level bias - the 'Labeling Bias' item about Mountbatten-Windsor is a stretch since that is his legal name. (4) It misses genuine author-prose bias instances like 'controversial plea agreement' (Subjective Qualifying Adjective), 'disgraced financier' (Word Choice), 'putting more holes in Andrew's assurances' (Spin/Opinion Presented as Fact), and 'lurid allegations' (Word Choice). The model's output systematically uses invented bias type labels and includes attribution rule violations.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Items 2 and 3 include quoted DOJ statements within the biasedText field. The DOJ quote about 'unfounded and false' claims and 'weaponised' language is attributed source material being flagged as author bias. Item 2 includes extensive quoted text from the DOJ.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Item 4 (Labeling Bias about Mountbatten-Windsor) is not a genuine bias instance - that's his legal name and the BBC commonly uses it. Items 2 and 3 largely flag editorial choices about space allocation and quoted material rather than clear author-prose bias. Item 1's claim about framing is somewhat defensible but overreaching.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Multiple clear author-prose bias instances were missed: 'disgraced financier' (Word Choice/Subjective Qualifying Adjective), 'controversial plea agreement' (Subjective Qualifying Adjective), 'putting more holes in Andrew's assurances' (Spin), 'sprawling sex trafficking case' (Word Choice), 'lurid allegations' (Word Choice).
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: All four bias type labels used ('Framing Bias', 'Emphasis Bias', 'Source Selection Bias', 'Labeling Bias') are not from the defined list of valid bias types. This is a complete failure in type labeling - none of the allowed types were used.
